The Three Pillars

Built on principles, proven on stage.

Every committee, case, and award at DYIO maps back to three commitments — the same three engraved on our seal: Innovation, Leadership, and Excellence.

Pillar — Innovation

Innovation

Delegates design and defend original ventures against live market shocks. Real constraints, real judges, real consequences — the boardroom, not the classroom. From ideation sprints to final pitch decks, innovation at DYIO is practiced, not preached.

Build · Pitch · Defend
Pillar — Leadership

Leadership

Teams are led, not managed. DYIO rewards the delegates who align people under pressure — negotiating deals, running crisis rooms, and carrying a strategy from the opening gavel to the final vote.

Direct · Negotiate · Decide
Pillar — Excellence

Excellence

From delegate kits to the closing ceremony, everything is held to an international standard — a 100% student-run event executed with professional discipline that schools, parents, and sponsors can trust.

Prepare · Perform · Prevail
What is DYIO

A launchpad, not just a competition.

Founded and run entirely by students, DYIO brings 500+ delegates from 30+ schools to Dubai for two days of business, economics, and innovation challenges — a melting pot where the region's next founders are forged.

Delegates step into the roles of founders, executives, economists, and investors. They tackle case studies, respond to live market shocks, negotiate deals, and pitch original ventures to real judges — building the exact skills that classrooms alone can't teach: public speaking, financial literacy, negotiation, and grace under pressure.

Read the full story →

The Arena

Three tracks. One stage.

Every delegate competes inside one of three core tracks — each with its own committees, cases, and awards.

Track 01

Business

Boardroom-style case battles. Delegates act as founders and executives, dissecting real company dilemmas — pricing, expansion, turnaround — and defending their strategy in front of a judging panel.

Track 02

Economics

Live market simulations. Committees respond to policy shocks, inflation spirals, and trade disputes in real time, balancing national interest against market forces.

Explore the track →
Track 03

Innovation & Shark Tank

The flagship. Teams build an original venture across both days and pitch it in the Shark Tank Arena — DYIO's highest-stakes stage, judged live in front of the full assembly.

Home/About

The story behind DYIO.

How a group of Dubai students built the region's most ambitious youth innovation olympiad — and why Innovation, Leadership, and Excellence are engraved on its seal.

The Dubai Youth Innovation Olympiad (DYIO) is a premier student-led business, economics, and innovation competition hosted in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. On September 7–8, 2026, DYIO will bring together more than 500 delegates from over 30 schools for two days of high-intensity competition, workshops, and networking — every part of it designed, organized, and executed by students.

Why DYIO exists

Most academic competitions test what students already know. DYIO was founded on a different conviction: that the defining skills of the next generation — building a venture under constraint, negotiating when the stakes are real, presenting a strategy to a skeptical room — can only be learned by doing. Classrooms teach the theory of business and economics; DYIO puts students inside it.

An Innovation Olympiad is a competitive simulation of the real business world. Delegates step into the roles of founders, executives, economists, and investors. They dissect case studies drawn from real companies, respond to live market shocks injected mid-committee, negotiate deals across the table, and defend original ventures in front of judging panels. It is a platform where critical thinking meets creativity — and where confidence is built the only way it ever really is: under pressure, in public, with something at stake.

For schools and parents, DYIO is among the most valuable extracurriculars a student can undertake. The skills it develops — entrepreneurship, public speaking, financial literacy, negotiation, teamwork, research, and leadership — compound across every subject a student studies and every application they will ever write.

Founded and run by students

DYIO is 100% student-founded and student-operated. The olympiad was created by Aadhavan (Founder & CEO), Azura (Co-Founder & CSO), and Ayaan (Co-Founder), and is run day-to-day by a full secretariat spanning marketing and media, finance, public relations, creative design, events, logistics, and delegate relations. DYIO grows alongside its sister conference, DDS MUN, as part of an expanding family of student-run events in the UAE.

That student ownership is not a footnote — it is the point. Every sponsor deck, delegate kit, stage cue, and judging rubric is produced by the same generation the event serves. When delegates walk into DYIO, they are walking into proof of what students can execute at a professional standard. Meet the full team.

The three pillars

Innovation

Innovation at DYIO is a discipline, not a buzzword. Delegates ideate, prototype on paper, price, and position original ventures — then defend them against judges who probe unit economics, market sizing, and go-to-market strategy. The flagship Shark Tank Arena is where this pillar comes alive: a live, high-stakes pitch stage in front of the full assembly.

Leadership

Every committee at DYIO is an exercise in leading without authority. Delegates must align teammates they didn't choose, negotiate with rivals they can't control, and hold a strategy together while the scenario shifts underneath them. Awards recognize not just the loudest voice in the room, but the delegate who moved the room.

Excellence

Excellence is the operational standard that holds the other two pillars up. It shows in the details: delegate kits that arrive complete, sessions that start on time, judges who are briefed, ceremonies that run to the minute. DYIO is built to a standard that schools, parents, and sponsors can put their name against.

What happens across the two days

  • Opening Ceremony — keynotes, sponsor recognition, and the launch of all competition tracks before the full assembly of delegates, parents, and guests.
  • Competition Rounds — business case battles, economics simulations, and innovation pitch rounds across dedicated committee rooms. See the full competition portal.
  • Workshops & Panels — sessions hosted by industry guests and sponsors: a direct channel between delegates and the professional world.
  • Closing & Awards Ceremony — grand prize presentations, named sponsor awards, and final recognition before the full audience.

Who competes

DYIO is open to students in grades 8–12. Delegates come primarily from Dubai's leading international schools, joined by international delegations arranged through partner tour operators from outside the UAE. No prior competition experience is required — committees are structured so that first-time delegates can contribute from the opening session, while veterans find ceilings high enough to be genuinely tested.

Our commitment

To delegates: a fair arena, real feedback, and a stage worth preparing for. To schools: professional organization, transparent judging, and communication you can rely on. To parents: a safe, supervised, and genuinely formative two days. To partners: an audience of 500+ future founders and the operational discipline to represent your brand well.

DYIO 2026 takes place September 7–8, 2026, in Dubai. Registration is open — and the earlier a delegation confirms, the better its committee allocation.

Innovation. Leadership. Excellence. Not a slogan — a rubric. Everything DYIO does is judged against it, starting with DYIO itself.
500+Delegates expected
30+Schools represented

Home/Competitions

The Competition Portal.

Three tracks, dedicated committee rooms, live shocks, and judged finals — everything a delegate competes in across the two days of DYIO 2026.

Register for a TrackShark Tank Arena →
How it works

One delegate. One track. Two days to prove it.

Every DYIO delegate registers into one of three core tracks — Business, Economics, or Innovation. Each track runs its own committees, its own case material, and its own awards, but all three share the same DNA: real scenarios, live pressure, and judging panels that reward substance over theatre.

Committees are capped to keep the floor competitive and the feedback personal. Cases are written by the DYIO secretariat and reviewed for balance, so no school or curriculum has a built-in advantage. Mid-session shock injects — a sudden competitor move, a policy reversal, a funding freeze — are DYIO's signature mechanic: they test whether a strategy survives contact with reality, and they are where most awards are won and lost.

Track 01

Business — the boardroom track.

The Business track puts delegates inside the executive suite. Working in teams, delegates receive a case built around a real company dilemma — a stalled expansion, a collapsing margin, a hostile market entry — and a limited window to produce a defensible strategy.

What delegates do

  • Case battles — structured rounds where teams present competing strategies for the same dilemma and are cross-examined by judges and rival teams.
  • Negotiation rounds — supplier terms, joint ventures, and acquisition talks conducted live across the table.
  • Executive Q&A — a rapid-fire defense of the numbers behind every recommendation.

What judges reward

Clarity of logic, command of the case facts, realistic financials, and composure under cross-examination. A beautiful deck with hollow numbers loses to a plain one that survives scrutiny.

Track 02

Economics — the market floor.

The Economics track is a living simulation. Committees represent governments, central banks, and market blocs, and the scenario moves in real time: inflation data lands mid-debate, a trading partner imposes tariffs, a currency slides. Delegates must respond with policy that balances national interest against market confidence.

What delegates do

  • Policy simulations — set rates, taxes, and trade positions, then live with the consequences the engine returns.
  • Crisis committees — compressed, high-pressure sessions triggered by shock injects.
  • Communiqué drafting — negotiate joint statements where every clause is contested.

What judges reward

Economic reasoning delegates can explain in plain language, coalition-building across the room, and policy that anticipates second-order effects instead of reacting to headlines.

Track 03

Innovation — build it, then defend it.

The Innovation track runs across both days. Teams ideate and develop an original venture — problem, product, market, pricing, and go-to-market — with structured checkpoints where mentors and judges pressure-test the thinking. The track culminates on the main stage: the Shark Tank Arena, where finalist teams pitch live before the full assembly and a judging panel, defending their venture through open Q&A.

The Innovation track carries DYIO's top overall honor — the Grand Innovation Award — presented at the Closing Ceremony. It is the most demanding track on the card, and the one delegates talk about for years.

Day 1

Build

Team formation, problem selection, venture development, and the first mentor checkpoint. By evening, every team has a one-page venture thesis.

Day 2 · AM

Pressure-test

Judge clinics probe unit economics and market claims. Teams revise, rehearse, and lock the final pitch. Semi-final heats select the Arena finalists.

Day 2 · PM

The Arena

Finalists pitch live on the main stage. Open Q&A, live judging, and the Grand Innovation Award presented at the Closing Ceremony.

Awards

What's on the line.

Per committee

Best Delegate

The delegate who moved the room — awarded in every committee across all three tracks.

Per committee

Outstanding & Honourable

Runner-up recognitions rewarding sustained, substantive contribution across both days.

Track finals

Track Champions

The winning team in each track — Business, Economics, and Innovation — recognized on the main stage.

Overall

Grand Innovation Award

DYIO's top honor, presented at the Closing Ceremony. Sponsors can attach their name to it — see Title packages.

Eligibility: grades 8–12 · No prior experience required · Delegates choose a track preference at registration and allocations run first-confirmed, first-allocated.

Home/Competitions/Shark Tank Arena

The Shark Tank Arena.

DYIO's flagship stage. Real ventures, live judges, open Q&A, and the full assembly watching. This is where the Grand Innovation Award is won.

Compete in the ArenaSponsor the Arena

Every olympiad has a moment the whole event points toward. At DYIO, it's the Shark Tank Arena: the final afternoon of Day 2, main stage, house lights down, when finalist teams walk out to pitch the venture they've built across both days — live, unscripted beyond their opening, and judged in front of 500+ delegates.

What the Arena is

The Arena is the culmination of the Innovation track. Teams of 3–5 delegates spend Day 1 and the morning of Day 2 building an original venture: identifying a real problem, shaping a product, sizing a market, setting a price, and mapping a route to the first hundred customers. Mentor checkpoints and judge clinics pressure-test the work at every stage — so by the time a team reaches the stage, the idea has already survived three rounds of scrutiny.

Semi-final heats on Day 2 select the finalists. Then the Arena: a timed pitch, followed by open Q&A where judges probe whatever they choose — unit economics, competitive moats, founder priorities. There is nowhere to hide, which is exactly the point.

The format

  • The pitch — 5 minutes. Problem, solution, market, model, ask. Slides allowed; reading off them is fatal.
  • Judge Q&A — 7 minutes. Open cross-examination. Judges are briefed to test the weakest plank of every pitch.
  • Deliberation — live scoring. Judges score on a published rubric; results are consolidated before the Closing Ceremony.
  • The Award. The winning team receives the Grand Innovation Award — DYIO's highest honor — on stage at the Closing Ceremony.

The judging rubric

Problem & insight — 25%

Is the problem real, painful, and specific? Teams that begin from a genuine insight — something they've observed, measured, or lived — consistently outperform teams that begin from a technology looking for a use.

Solution & feasibility — 25%

Does the product actually solve the problem, and could this team plausibly build a first version? Judges reward scrappy, testable first steps over grand unbuildable visions.

Market & model — 25%

Who pays, how much, and why? Bottom-up market sizing beats headline numbers. A pitch that knows its unit economics — even roughly — stands out immediately.

Delivery & defense — 25%

The pitch itself, and the Q&A. Composure matters: judges remember the team that treated a hard question as a gift, not a threat.

How to prepare

Winning teams tend to do four things before they arrive: pick a problem they genuinely understand; talk to at least five potential users; know their three weakest numbers and prepare honest answers about them; and rehearse the pitch out loud until the opening sixty seconds are automatic. Our blog publishes preparation guides from the secretariat, and past finalists are profiled on the Winners page.

For sponsors

The Arena is the single most-watched moment of DYIO — full assembly, all eyes on one stage. Title and Platinum partners can attach their name directly to it: the Grand Prize becomes the "[Brand] Grand Innovation Award," presented on stage by your representative, with your branding on the media wall behind every pitch. See sponsorship packages or write to us for Arena-specific options.

Five minutes to pitch. Seven to survive. Two days to earn the right to try.
5 minThe pitch
7 minJudge Q&A
3–5Delegates per team
1Grand Innovation Award

Ready to face the Arena?

How a student secretariat runs a 500-delegate event

Every function you'd find in a professional events company exists inside DYIO — staffed by students, held to the same standard, and accountable to the same deadline: September 7, 2026, 9:00 AM, doors open.

Executive

The founders — Aadhavan, Azura, and Ayaan — set the olympiad's direction: the tracks on the card, the standards in the rubric, and the partnerships that fund it. Vivek, as Director General, translates that direction into an operating plan and chairs the secretariat through the year.

Finance

Aarav Mamtani (CFO) and Jvalin manage budgeting, sponsorship invoicing, and vendor payments. Every dirham of sponsor investment maps to a line item — venue, delegate kits, awards, production — and partners receive a post-event impact report.

Marketing & Media

Pranav (CMO) and Nareese run the channels that fill the room: the website, @dyio.ae on Instagram, school outreach campaigns, and the event-week content engine. If a delegate heard about DYIO, this department is why.

PR & Outreach

Emmanuel and Gautam manage school relations, guest invitations, and press — the human network of 30+ schools, principals, and coordinators that a competition of this scale runs on.

Creative Design

Ethan's department owns everything visual: the seal, stage design, delegate kits, certificates, and the material sponsors put their logos on. One brand, applied without exception.

Events & Logistics

Kaden, Haruto, and Amna turn the plan into a physical day: room allocation, AV, scheduling, catering coordination, signage, and the minute-by-minute run of both ceremonies. When a session starts on time, that's this team.

Delegate Relations, Staff & Jury

Sahein manages the delegate experience end-to-end — registration queries, allocations, day-of support — while Aiman and Ethan coordinate the volunteer staff corps and the judging panels that keep every committee fair.

DYIO recruits secretariat members and volunteers from across Dubai's schools after each edition. Follow @dyio.ae for application windows, or write to aadhavan.4449@aiadubai.com.

Why sponsor DYIO

DYIO 2026 gathers more than 500 students in grades 8–12 from 30+ leading schools — with parents, educators, and school leadership alongside them. It is a rare, concentrated audience: young, ambitious, digitally native, and unusually attentive to the brands that back them.

  • Targeted exposure — recognition across the venue, the event programme, delegate communications, and DYIO's digital channels before, during, and after the event.
  • Tomorrow's customers — delegates are future consumers, university applicants, and founders; sponsorship builds affinity years before your competitors reach them.
  • CSR that shows — backing a youth-led educational initiative is a visible, credible Corporate Social Responsibility commitment in the UAE.
  • Community access — relationships with 30+ schools, education organizations, and fellow sponsors in Dubai's education ecosystem.
  • Cross-event reach — Platinum and Title partners extend to DDS MUN and all DYIO sister events for a full year.

Custom partnerships

Home/Delegations

Bring your country to Dubai.

Official partnerships for tour operators, educational travel companies, and schools outside the UAE — build a complete academic travel product around DYIO 2026.

Become a Partner OperatorWhatsApp Us

DYIO welcomes delegations from around the world. We partner with tour operators and educational travel companies outside the UAE who bring student groups from their home countries to compete at DYIO 2026 — a flagship international competition that anchors a complete Dubai academic travel package.

Why DYIO works as a travel product

Educational travel sells best when the itinerary has a centerpiece — a reason the trip happens in that city, that week. DYIO gives operators exactly that: two structured competition days (September 7–8, 2026) with ceremonies, judged finals, certificates, and awards, wrapped in one of the world's most visitable cities. Parents buy outcomes, schools buy structure, and students want the stage. The sightseeing sells itself.

Delegates compete in English across three tracks — Business, Economics, and Innovation — against 500+ peers from 30+ schools. International delegations are welcomed by name at the Opening Ceremony, and every delegate leaves with a certificate; winners leave with rather more.

How the partnership works

Step 01You recruitPromote DYIO to schools and students in your market and assemble a delegation — 10+ students, any mix of grades 8–12, with accompanying chaperones.
Step 02You package the tripBundle flights, hotels, transfers, and Dubai sightseeing around the two competition days. It's your product and your margin — DYIO stays out of your travel economics.
Step 03We hostDYIO handles registration, committee allocation, delegate kits, meals during event hours, certificates, and awards for your entire group — with a dedicated liaison from the secretariat.

What partner operators receive

  • Official Delegation Partner status — exclusive appointment as DYIO's official operator for your country or region, listed on this website and in event materials.
  • Group registration discounts — preferential delegate fees for groups, plus complimentary chaperone passes. Margin you keep or pass on.
  • Priority allocation & onboarding — reserved committee seats, early registration windows, and a named liaison inside the secretariat.
  • Co-branded marketing — joint promotional materials, features on @dyio.ae, and licensed use of the DYIO name in your travel packages.
  • On-stage recognition — your delegations welcomed by name at the Opening Ceremony, with the operator credited in the event programme.

Commercial models

Three structures, all negotiable, all scalable with delegation size:

  • Per-delegate commission — a set commission on every registered delegate you bring. Simplest to start.
  • Wholesale group rates — discounted block registration you resell inside your package at your own price.
  • Annual country licence — exclusive rights to your market across DYIO and sister events for twelve months, with a committed delegate volume.

Terms are agreed in a short written partnership memo before any promotion begins — clean, simple, and signed by both sides.

For schools abroad without an operator

Schools outside the UAE can also register a delegation directly. Write to us with your school name, expected group size, and grades, and we'll either onboard you directly or connect you with our partner operator in your region — whichever gets your students to Dubai more smoothly.

Start the conversation

Email aadhavan.4449@aiadubai.com with your company profile, market, and expected group size — or message us on WhatsApp. We typically respond within 48 hours with a draft partnership structure for your market.

Dubai Youth Innovation Olympiad (DYIO) – Delegate Terms & Conditions

1. Registration, Confirmation & Cancellation Fee: By submitting this registration form, the applicant formally confirms their spot for the September 2026 DYIO Shark Tank Competition. Due to the limited availability of slots and elite nature of the event, any cancellation made after submission will be subject to a cancellation fee, and the 350 AED Early Bird fee remains non-refundable.

2. September 2026 Event Format: The September 7–8 event will follow a competitive "Shark Tank" presentation and pitching format. Date and premium Dubai venue announcements are pending and will be officially communicated via email and our official channels.

3. Team Formations: Solo applicants registering under "Team Placement Needed" accept that DYIO organizers hold final authority on team matching and peer placement based on skills and profiles.

4. Code of Conduct: All delegates, coaches, and institutional representatives are expected to maintain professional entrepreneurship standards. Plagiarism, structural copying of existing commercial intellectual property, or unsportsmanlike behavior will result in immediate disqualification without a refund.

5. Media Release: By participating, delegates grant DYIO the right to use event photography, pitch recordings, and presentation snippets for promotional and media archive purposes.

Questions? WhatsApp us or DM @dyio.ae

How registration works

Three ways in: through your school (recommended), as an individual delegate, or as an international delegation via a partner operator. All three end the same way — a confirmed seat, a committee allocation, and a briefing pack in your inbox.

Step 01Send your detailsEmail or WhatsApp us with: delegate name(s), school, grade(s), and track preference — Business, Economics, or Innovation. School coordinators send the full delegation list in one message.
Step 02Confirm your seatWe reply with fees, payment details, and available committees. Your seat is locked on payment confirmation — and allocation priority is set by that timestamp.
Step 03Receive your briefingCommittee allocation, case briefing, schedule, and venue details arrive by email in the weeks before the event. Innovation track delegates also receive the venture-building guide.
Step 04CompeteSeptember 7–8, 2026. Doors open in the morning, the Opening Ceremony launches all tracks, and two days later somebody lifts the Grand Innovation Award.

What every delegate receives

  • The delegate kit — event materials, schedule, notebook, badge, and sponsor items, handed over at check-in.
  • Meals during event hours — lunch and refreshments on both days.
  • Certificate of participation — presented to every delegate; award certificates are presented on stage.
  • Award eligibility — Best Delegate, Outstanding and Honourable mentions in every committee, track championships, and the Grand Innovation Award. See what's on the line.

Dates that matter

  • Now — registration open; early confirmation improves track and committee allocation.
  • August 2026 — committee allocations and case briefings released to confirmed delegates.
  • September 7–8, 2026 — DYIO 2026, Dubai.
FAQ

Everything delegates ask us.

Who is eligible to compete at DYIO 2026?
Students in grades 8–12 (or equivalent) at the time of the event. Delegates register through their school where possible; individual registrations are also accepted. International students join via partner tour operators or direct school delegations.
When and where does DYIO 2026 take place?
September 7–8, 2026, in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. The venue will be announced to registered delegates and schools first. Both days run from morning to late afternoon, opening and closing with full-assembly ceremonies.
Do I need prior competition experience?
No. Committees are structured so first-time delegates can contribute from the opening session — case materials and briefing guides are sent in advance. Experienced delegates will find the ceilings high enough to be genuinely tested.
How do track allocations work?
You state a track preference — Business, Economics, or Innovation — at registration. Allocations run first-confirmed, first-allocated, so early registration meaningfully improves your chance of a preferred track and committee.
What does registration include?
Full participation across both days, the delegate kit, meals and refreshments during event hours, a certificate of participation, and eligibility for all committee and track awards.
Can schools register a group?
Yes — schools are our primary registration channel. A coordinating teacher submits the delegation list and receives consolidated communication, invoicing, and allocations. Chaperone passes are provided for accompanying staff.
How do international students register?
Through a DYIO partner tour operator in your country where one exists, or directly as a school delegation. See the Delegations page or email us and we'll route you the fastest way.
What should delegates prepare before arriving?
Read the case briefing sent to your committee, know your track's format, and — for Innovation track delegates — arrive with two or three problem areas you genuinely understand. Our blog publishes preparation guides throughout the year.

Something we didn't cover? Email aadhavan.4449@aiadubai.com or message +971 58 546 3882 on WhatsApp — we typically reply within 48 hours.

Seats are allocated by timestamp.

The earlier you confirm, the better your committee. Send your details today and lock your track before allocations tighten.

Home/Winners

Where names become precedents.

Winners, awards, and highlights from the DYIO stage — and the honor roll that DYIO 2026 will be written into this September.

The honors board

Every DYIO edition closes the same way: the full assembly seated, the stage lights up, and one by one the names are read. Committee awards first, then the track championships, and finally the Grand Innovation Award — the single highest honor on the card. This page is where those names live afterward.

DYIO 2026 will crown winners across every level of the competition. Here is what's at stake, and how each honor is earned:

Best Delegate — every committee

Awarded in every committee across all three tracks to the delegate who moved the room: the strongest sustained combination of preparation, argument, and floor leadership across both days. Judges score continuously — a brilliant final session cannot rescue two silent days.

Outstanding & Honourable Mentions

The depth awards. Outstanding Delegate recognizes consistent, substantive contribution just short of the top spot; Honourable Mentions credit delegates whose specific interventions — a negotiation rescued, a crisis read correctly — shaped their committee's outcome.

Track Champions

The winning team in each of the three tracks — Business, Economics, and Innovation — recognized on the main stage at the Closing Ceremony. Track championships weigh the judged finals most heavily, with committee performance as the tiebreaker.

The Grand Innovation Award

DYIO's top overall honor, won in the Shark Tank Arena. The winning team's venture is the one that survived every checkpoint, every clinic, and seven minutes of open cross-examination in front of 500 people. Title sponsors can attach their name to this award — see how.

Highlights from the DYIO stage

The moments that define an edition rarely fit in a results table. A first-time delegate taking Best Delegate off veterans twice her age in committee experience. A team rebuilding its entire pitch overnight after a brutal Day 1 clinic — and winning the Arena with the new one. A crisis committee that negotiated through lunch because nobody would concede the room. These are the stories the honors board only hints at, and they're told in full on our blog and on @dyio.ae.

For schools: what winning here means

DYIO awards are earned against 30+ schools and 500+ delegates under published rubrics and independent judging panels — which is exactly why they carry weight. Schools receive consolidated results for their delegations after the event, and award-winning delegates are named in DYIO's post-event announcements. For a student's university file, a DYIO honor is evidence, not decoration: leadership, demonstrated in public, under pressure, against real competition.

DYIO 2026 — the board is open

The 2026 honors board will be written on September 8, at the Closing Ceremony in Dubai. Every name on it will belong to a delegate who confirmed a seat, prepared properly, and performed when it counted. Registration is open — and history books don't hold seats.

Results are announced once, on stage, to everyone. That's the way it should feel.

Home/Blog

Insights from the secretariat.

Preparation guides, strategy, and stories from the people who build DYIO — written for delegates who intend to win.

Guide · Delegates

How to win your committee at DYIO (without being the loudest voice)

Every edition, the delegates who take Best Delegate share the same three habits — and none of them is volume. Here's what judges actually score, from the people who brief the judges.

1. Own the case before the case owns you. Your committee's briefing pack arrives weeks before the event. The winning move is boring: read it twice, list the five facts everything turns on, and write one page in your own words on what you'd do. Delegates who arrive with a position adapt under shocks; delegates who arrive planning to "read the room" get read by it.

2. Score in the transitions. Most delegates perform in speeches and disappear between them. Judges watch the in-between: who builds the coalition during the unmoderated caucus, who drafts the clause both sides can sign, who asks the question that reframes the debate. At DYIO, shock injects land mid-session precisely to create these transitions — treat every disruption as a scoring window, because that's how it's designed.

3. Make your opponents better. Counterintuitive but consistent: Best Delegates elevate the room. They steelman the other side before dismantling it, credit good points by name, and negotiate as if the deal matters more than the credit. Judges are briefed to distinguish dominance from leadership — and the rubric pays for leadership.

Do these three things across both days and you'll be in every award conversation, whatever your track. See how each track scores in detail on the Competition Portal.

Guide · Shark Tank

The anatomy of a winning Arena pitch

Five minutes to pitch, seven to survive Q&A. Past finalists' pitches share a skeleton — steal it.

Minute 1 — the problem, made personal. Open with the specific moment your problem exists: a person, a place, a cost. Judges hear market sizes all day; they remember the pitch that made them feel the problem first and quantified it second.

Minutes 2–3 — the solution and the model. Show the product in one sentence, then spend your time where amateur pitches don't: who pays, how much, and what it costs you to serve them. A rough unit-economics line — "we make eleven dirhams on every fifteen-dirham sale" — outscores a polished feature tour every time.

Minute 4 — proof. Anything real: five user conversations, a waitlist, a paper prototype someone tried. The Arena rubric gives 25% to feasibility, and proof is how feasibility sounds.

Minute 5 — the ask and the close. End on exactly what you'd do next with resources, and land the last sentence you rehearsed. Then the real event begins: Q&A. Prepare honest answers to your three weakest numbers before you walk on — judges are briefed to find the weakest plank, and the teams that win treat the hardest question as the best moment of their pitch.

Full format, rubric, and preparation checklist on the Shark Tank Arena page.

Essay · Behind DYIO

Why a student-run olympiad hits different

DYIO's secretariat is entirely students — finance, logistics, judging operations, all of it. That isn't a limitation we work around. It's the product.

When adults run a youth competition, students attend an event. When students run it, delegates walk into an argument: proof that people their age can carry budgets, brief judges, negotiate with vendors, and put a thousand people through two flawless days. Every delegate spends the weekend inside evidence of what's possible — and a meaningful number of them leave asking how to join the secretariat.

It changes the competition itself, too. Cases are written by people who remember being bored by textbook cases. Shock injects are timed by people who know exactly when a committee's energy dips. The judging rubric rewards what students actually respect in each other — substance, composure, generosity under pressure — because the people who wrote it compete in these rooms themselves.

And it changes what sponsorship means. A brand that backs DYIO isn't buying a logo on someone else's production; it's underwriting a working demonstration of youth capability, run to a professional standard, in front of the exact audience that will remember it. That's the argument we make on our Partners page — and the event is the evidence.

Innovation, Leadership, Excellence. The pillars aren't aspirations we set for delegates. They're the standard we hold ourselves to first.

Guide · Parents & Schools

A parent's guide to DYIO: what two days actually buy

The honest version of why 30+ schools send delegations — and what to expect if your child competes.

What it builds. Across two days a delegate will speak publicly at least a dozen times, negotiate with strangers, defend numbers under questioning, and work in a team they didn't choose. These are the skills interviews and universities actually probe — and they compound in ways one more test score doesn't.

What it evidences. DYIO honors are earned against 500+ delegates under published rubrics and independent judges. On an application, "Best Delegate, DYIO 2026" is a verifiable claim about leadership under pressure — evidence, not adjectives.

What it costs. Registration covers both days, the delegate kit, meals during event hours, and award eligibility — full details on the registration page. The real investment is preparation time, and the briefing pack makes that time efficient.

What it feels like. Long, loud, occasionally overwhelming, and — by the Closing Ceremony — the kind of weekend students describe as the moment something clicked. Follow @dyio.ae during the event; the faces tell you more than we can.

Read enough. Register.

Preparation guides only pay off with a confirmed seat. Lock your track, get your briefing pack, and put the reading to work.
